note.soap:
S: CC: refill blood pressure medication | Age band: 18-64 | Medication: lisinopril | Previously prescribed: yes | Side effects concern: no | New/worse symptoms: no
O: Phone encounter. No vitals available.
A: Draft assessment pending protocol/physician routing.

patient_instructions: If symptoms worsen or you develop chest pain, severe trouble breathing, confusion, fainting, or severe bleeding, seek emergency care.
Follow up if not improving as expected.
clinician_questions: Review and finalize assessment and plan (prototype).
Confirm whether any testing, in-person exam, or medication action is appropriate.

evidence.title: Evidence excerpt
evidence.snippet:
TITLE: Demo Triage Guidance (Prototype)

GENERAL SAFETY:
- If chest pain, severe trouble breathing, stroke symptoms, severe bleeding, fainting, or active self-harm thoughts: call EMS.

UPPER RESPIRATORY INFECTION (URI) - ADULT:
- Typical viral URI: sore throat, runny nose, mild cough, low-grade fever, symptoms < 10 days.
- Self-care: hydration, res

evidence.snippet:
days, severe sore throat with high fever, or significant comorbidities.

URINARY SYMPTOMS (possible uncomplicated cystitis) - ADULT FEMALE:
- Typical: dysuria, frequency, urgency, suprapubic discomfort, no flank pain, no fever.
- Urgent evaluation today if: fever, flank pain, vomiting, pregnancy, or severe systemic symptoms.
- Otherwise: clinic eva
